title: Spring Rolls

ingredients.title: For the Wrappers:
items: Alternatively, use store-bought spring roll wrappers for convenience

ingredients.title: For the Filling:
items: 200g ground pork or chicken (optional for vegetarian: use tofu or mushrooms)
1 tsp oyster sauce (or vegetarian mushroom sauce)

instruction.title: Prepare the Wrappers (if making from scratch):
steps: Mix flour, salt, and water to form a smooth, pliable dough.
Knead for 5–8 minutes until elastic. Cover with damp cloth and rest 30 minutes.
Divide dough into small balls (~1.5 inches). Roll each into thin circles or squares (1–2 mm thick).

instruction.title: Make the Filling:
steps: Heat 1 tsp oil in a pan over medium heat.
Add garlic, ginger, and green onions; sauté until fragrant.
Add meat (if using) and cook until browned.
Add cabbage, carrots, mushrooms, and bean sprouts; stir-fry 2–3 minutes until slightly tender but still crisp.
Season with soy sauce, oyster sauce, sesame oil, and white pepper.
Sprinkle cornstarch over mixture, stir well, then remove from heat and cool completely.

instruction.title: Assemble the Spring Rolls:
steps: Place 2 tablespoons of filling near the corner of a wrapper.
Fold the corner over filling, fold the sides in, then roll tightly.
Seal edge with a dab of water or beaten egg.

instruction.title: Fry the Spring Rolls:
steps: Fry spring rolls in batches until golden brown and crispy, about 3–5 minutes.

chefTips: Double-fry for extra crispiness: fry once lightly, cool, then fry again until golden.
Don’t overfill wrappers to ensure tight rolling and even cooking.
Add Chinese rice wine or fish sauce for deeper umami flavor.
Serve immediately with sweet chili sauce, plum sauce, or soy-vinegar dip.
